meaning: n.飞快的移动v.(使)飞快移动int.(飞速行进等发出的声音)嗖,呼,咳
examples: Douglas Adams famously said he loved the "whoosh" of missed deadlines passing over his head.
There was a whoosh as everything went up in flames.
Then came the riders amid even louder cheers and whoosh! It was all over.
The motor uses a whoosh of air to push its two pistons up and down.
What you need is a whoosh of water--that's money supply--combined with some Drano--that's fiscal restructuring.
